title: AAIB investigation to ATR 72-212 A, G-IACZ

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Aircraft diverted because the flaps could not be fully extended on approach to Cornwall Airport, Newquay, 15 April 2025 The flaps could not be deployed to full extension for landing and the commander decided to perform a go-around. Weather conditions were unfavourable so after initially declaring a Mayday, the aircraft diverted to Exeter Airport where landing flap was achieved. The landing was uneventful, but several unrelated anomalies were reported to have occurred during the subsequent ground taxi.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: ATR 72-212 A, G-IACZ 11-25 Download glossary of abbreviations: Glossary of abbreviations The AAIB record identifies the aircraft as a ATR 72-212 A. The registration listed is G-IACZ. The occurrence location is given as On approach to Cornwall Airport, Newquay . The date of occurrence is recorded as 2025-04-15. The report type is correspondence-investigation. The aircraft category is commercial-fixed-wing.
